seed_problem: Assign agents to tasks; the value of an assignment is a submodular coverage over task-features (diminishing returns), not a linear sum; maximize coverage.
mutation: composite: keep the assignment-x-submodularity goal but COUPLE it with a geometric energy knapsack (each drone->zone pairing costs a rounded Euclidean flight energy, global budget E) and hard zone capacities. The value is a SATURATING (min-capped) feature coverage summed over zones, so it is submodular; a tight energy budget forces choosing ~a-dozen drones out of many while feature overlap makes greedy-by-marginal saturate and waste budget, so a coordinated swap/spread wins.
title: Nimbus Swarm: Submodular Airspace Coverage
strategies: trivial baseline: deploy the single most valuable feasible (drone,zone) pairing
greedy-by-absolute-marginal: repeatedly add the pairing with the largest coverage gain that fits budget and capacity (falls into the feature-overlap saturation trap)
ratio greedy: add the pairing with the best gain-per-energy, spending the tight budget more efficiently
randomized local search: ratio-greedy seed then add/swap/move/remove moves that spread complementary drones and reclaim energy from saturated picks
